title: neuro.correlogram request
description: GENERATED from contract/skills/neuro.correlogram.v1.json. The acceptance authority for this skill.

oneOf.description: Raw events for the correlogram. Both trains are observed over the SAME window and pooled into one time-ordered stream; the per-event sender ids identify which sender produced each event, and the recorded-sender universe is the complete set of senders (including silent ones) that were recorded.

eventSenderIds.description: Parallel to eventTimes: the sender that produced each event.

recordedSenderIds.description: The COMPLETE set of senders that were recorded across both trains, including any that never fired. Every event sender must be a member of it.

referenceEventCount.description: The number of reference events inside the window. It is the per-reference-event denominator basis and is required for a correlation coefficient.

targetEventCount.description: The number of target events inside the window.

effectiveDuration.description: The effective observation duration, DERIVED as stop - start from the window when raw events are supplied, or declared explicitly here for a pre-binned figure. It is the exposure the per-reference-event rate divides by.

oneOf.description: A pre-binned correlogram, for example a NEST correlation-detector status. Cortexel did not observe the events and cannot re-bin them; it re-derives and checks the normalized value from the integer pair counts and the declared denominators.

binEdges.description: The LAG bin edges, ordered from -tauMax to +tauMax. n edges define n-1 half-open lag bins; the zero-lag bin is centred on zero.

counts.description: Exact integer pair counts per lag bin. This is the auditable numerator; a normalized value that cannot be re-derived from it is refused. Length must be edges.length - 1.

rates.description: Optional. The target rate per reference event as the source computed it. If supplied it is RE-DERIVED from the pair counts and the declared denominator and checked; it is never taken on trust.

recordedSenderCount.description: The per-reference-event denominator basis (the reference event count under edge correction none). It cannot be recovered from the pair counts.

referenceEventCount.description: The number of reference events, required for a correlation coefficient.

effectiveDuration.description: The effective observation duration, DERIVED as stop - start from the window when raw events are supplied, or declared explicitly here for a pre-binned figure. It is the exposure the per-reference-event rate divides by.

selfPairTreatment.description: `excluded_by_source`: shared events existed and the source removed their self-pairs. `no_shared_event_identity`: no event can appear in both trains, so no self-pair exists to exclude. A source that shared events and KEPT their self-pairs cannot be labelled honestly and is refused.

pairScope.description: One reference train against one target train. `matched_pairs` and `all_pairs` are refused: they yield many correlograms or a pooled statistic with a different denominator.

lagRange.description: The lag axis extent. lag = t_target - t_reference; a positive lag means the target follows the reference. min is -tauMax and max is +tauMax; max must exceed min.

bins.description: The lag bin width. It must be positive and must tile the lag range; smoothing is never offered, so the only way to change the temporal resolution is to declare a different width.

statistic.description: The per-bin quantity. `raw_pair_count` has no denominator. `target_rate_per_reference_event` divides by the reference events and the bin width. `correlation_coefficient` is the Pearson correlation of the two binned trains and requires the per-train event counts and an effective duration; it is refused when those are absent.

edgeCorrection.description: `none` leaves the finite-window taper in the figure, disclosed. `eligible_reference_events` divides by the reference events whose lag-shifted bin lies inside the window. `valid_overlap_only` is the Pearson variant over the overlapping bins.

normalization.description: How a pre-binned rate was formed, so it can be re-derived and checked. `mean_rate_per_recorded_sender` divides the pair count by the reference-event denominator and the bin width; `total_event_rate` divides by the bin width alone.
